theme: Maximize satisfied constraint weight in a weighted CSP with a planted backbone hidden among free variables.
title: The Quiet Chain: Weighted Constraints with a Hidden Backbone
innovation_hook: Probe the backbone by propagating forced literals from the heaviest constraints, freeze those variables, and focus stochastic flips only on the free set.
strategies: trivial: static per-variable weighted-majority vote (no search) -- reproduces the evaluator's own baseline exactly (~0.1)

greedy (the trap): plain WalkSAT over ALL variables uniformly, greedy break/make flips with noise, a few full random restarts -- handles the numerous light noise clauses fine but, having no notion that some variables matter more, drifts backbone variables toward whatever the light decoy clauses locally prefer, losing the rare-but-heavy anchor clauses that need those same variables at the opposite value

strong (the insight): probe the backbone by running unit propagation restricted to the heaviest clauses (found via the largest gap in the sorted weight spectrum, not a hardcoded constant) -- heavy unit clauses pin a variable directly, heavy 2-literal clauses chain-propagate the rest; freeze the recovered backbone, then run focused WalkSAT + a short tabu list + periodic free-only restarts on just the remaining variables

headroom above strong: exact/near-exact MAX-SAT solving on the free-variable subproblem (e.g. ILP relaxation, better local-search schedules, smarter tabu tenure tuning) to squeeze more of the light-clause weight beyond what a fixed-budget WalkSAT recovers
